title: Canine Respiratory Pathogen Detection Assays
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5f4r6","text":"The Canine Respiratory Pathogen (CRP) Detection Assays is intended as an in vitro veterinary reagent set, based on Reverse Transcription quantitative PCR (RT-qPCR), for the detection of canine adenovirus-2 (CAdV-2), canine distemper (CDV), canine herpesvirus type 1 (CHV-1), canine influenza A virus (CIV), canine parainfluenza (CPiV), canine pneumovirus (CPnV), canine respiratory coronavirus (CRCoV), SARS-CoV-2, Bordetella bronchiseptica, Streptococcus equi subsp. zooepidemicus, Mycoplasma cynos and Mycoplasma canis in nasal and pharyngeal swab samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":73,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":415,"length":25},{"style":"italic","offset":442,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":468,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":483,"length":17},{"style":"italic","offset":504,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5f6gh","text":"Shipping and Storage ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ap8gm","text":"The CRP Detection Assays are shipped on dry ice. Reagents should arrive frozen. The Reagents in the purple and red tubes may arrive liquid, this will not result in a reduction in performance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8h7d8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fsrfs","text":"All reagents should be stored at -20°C upon arrival. All reagents can be stored for a minimum of one year (from the date of shipment) at -20°C without showing a reduction in performance. Positive controls should be stored at  -80°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5u9e1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aflqh","text":"Limitation:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"afdht","text":"Strict compliance with the instructions is required for optimal results.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"apm4i","text":"Appropriate specimen collection, transport, storage, and processing procedures are required for the optimal performance of this test.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b8mvl","text":"The presence of RT-PCR inhibitors may cause false negatives.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1nhqk","text":"Results of Canine Influenza A Subtypes Identification Assay need to be interpreted in consideration of all clinical and laboratory findings.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c7hug","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9q1tg","text":"Quality Control:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"62q82","text":"The specificity of each test was validated using a panel of reference and related canine respiratory pathogens.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7rdsf","text":"The analytical sensitivity of each assay was determined using ten-fold dilution of in vitro transcribed RNA or plasmid copies number. All assays have a limit of detection (LOD95)  60 copies/l.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":175,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"al8vv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fad1t","text":"Assay description and components ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"92aod","text":"The reagents are assembled for 60 reactions (+ 10% extra).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85dm7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"prr7x","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"83kt6","text":"* 3 tubes of primers \u0026 probes and positive controls are provided and correspond to the canine respiratory assays CRA_1, CRA_2, and CRA_3.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dvu6g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a804m","text":"Probe dye setting ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"48dhf","text":" TaqMan QSYTM Probe set are used as follows:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":11,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0s81y","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"sasbo","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":{}},{"key":"5ll0b","text":"Material and equipment required but NOT provided. nucleic acid extraction instrument and kits","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8132r","text":"Appropriate real-time PCR instrument calibrated for ABYTM, FAMTM, JUNTM and VICTM dyes (e.g., Applied Biosystems 7500 Fast Real-time PCR machine)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":55,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":62,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":69,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":79,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6a4vm","text":"Vortex and benchtop centrifuge","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1n6ai","text":"Appropriate 96-well reaction plate or reaction tubes with corresponding closing tape or caps","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"818ne","text":"Pipettes \u0026 steps: Centrifuge all reagents on a benchtop centrifuge to ensure no liquid is in cap and keep on ice The CRP Detection Reagents do not include an internal control, but positive controls are provided for each of the three assays (CRA_1, CRA_2 and CRA_3). A positive and a negative control should be run simultaneously with each sample setup.
The following fluorescence channels should be selected: ABY TM , FAM TM , JUN TM , and VIC TM .
ROX TM should be used as a passive reference dye.

The standard mode should be selected. Setup cycling condition following table 2: A B C D 1 Step Number of cycles Temp. (°C) Time (min:sec) 2 Reverse transcriptase 1 50 20:00 3 Initial activation 1 95 15:00 4 Denaturation 40 94 00:45 5 Annealing/extension 60 00:75 Table 2. Thermal profile. The data acquisition is performed during the annealing/extension step.
Before results analysis, the threshold value of each fluorescent dye must be manually set in the region of exponential amplification, typically 0.1 × ΔRn value at the plateau phase.
Each assay is considered valid if the following criteria are met: Table 3. Assays criteria
The results are qualitative (Positive or Negative). A specimen is considered positive if the Ct value obtained is below the following Ct cut-off values: Table 4. Ct cut-off values
Setup the Master Mix according to the following table 1: Table 1.
